selectedRows.reason: Selected as a future proof candidate because it is conditional on touch input and does not directly remove cursor visibility at apply time.
Selected as a future proof candidate because it is conditional on tablet input and does not directly remove cursor visibility at apply time.

rejectedRows.reason: Excluded first because it can remove cursor visibility immediately and globally; requires explicit later approval.
Rejected for first subset because numeric timing can hide cursor quickly; needs bound-specific proof and warning design first.
Rejected for first visibility subset because it can hide cursor during keyboard confirmation; needs dedicated CLI-token usability proof.

selectionRules: prefer conditional visibility policy over always-invisible policy
prefer boolean rows over numeric timing rows
prefer rows that do not instantly remove cursor visibility
avoid cursor.invisible first unless explicitly approved later
avoid rows where current value could make cursor immediately disappear without delay
require full recovery independence from cursor/mouse/UI/keybinds/pointer focus/workspace focus
futureProofRequired: UI/review projection with cursor visibility warning
no active runtime mutation during tests
restore command must be shown before applying
warning must explain cursor may disappear or become hard to use
confirmation path must not require clicking keep with a visible cursor
timeout restore must be automatic if user cannot interact
result log must record whether confirmation or automatic restore occurred
noEnablementNotice: Selected rows are for future proof planning only. They remain high-risk blocked.
